name: Parque Natural de Penyagolosa — Circular desde Xodos hasta Puertomingalvo
region_intro: Mountain biking around Xodos / Chodos offers challenging routes through varied terrain. The region is characterized by mountainous landscapes, significant elevation changes, and natural park areas, providing a robust environment for mountain biking. Riders can expect long ascents and descents, often traversing through forests and open country. The area's topography makes it suitable for experienced mountain bikers seeking demanding trails. Best mountain bike trails around Xodos / Chodos The most popular mountain bike trail is Parque Natural de Penyagolosa — Circular desde Xodos hasta Puertomingalvo, a 34.2 miles (55.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 40 minutes to complete. This circular route explores the natural park with significant climbs. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Molino de Carrasco – Atzeneta del Maestrat loop from Atzeneta del Maestrat, a difficult 28.8 miles (46.4 km) path. This route features challenging ascents and descents through the local landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Serra d'en Galceran – Collado de la Bandereta loop from Atzeneta del Maestrat, a 51.5 miles (82.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 7 hours 40 minutes. Mountain biking around Xodos / Chodos is defined by mountainous terrain, challenging ascents, and dense forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, including several moderate and many difficult routes. The routes in Xodos / Chodos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from a few reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Xodos / Chodos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Xodos / Chodos?
A: There are 29 mountain bike trails documented on komoot around Xodos / Chodos. These routes cater primarily to experienced riders, with 23 classified as difficult and 6 as moderate.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Xodos / Chodos?
A: The mountain biking routes around Xodos / Chodos are generally challenging, with a focus on difficult and moderate trails. There are no routes specifically classified as easy for beginners in this area on komoot.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Xodos / Chodos?
A: You can expect mountainous landscapes with significant elevation changes, long ascents and descents, and trails often traversing through dense forests and open country. The topography provides a robust environment for experienced mountain bikers.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Xodos / Chodos area?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931968043/parque-natural-de-penyagolosa-circular-desde-xodos-hasta-puertomingalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Penyagolosa — Circular desde Xodos hasta Puertomingalvo&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail exploring the natural park, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40363525" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino de Carrasco – Atzeneta del Maestrat loop from Atzeneta del Maestrat&lt;/a&gt; also offers a challenging circular experience.

Q: What are some of the longer mountain bike trails around Xodos / Chodos?
A: For those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31448745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra d'en Galceran – Collado de la Bandereta loop from Atzeneta del Maestrat&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 51.5-mile (82.8 km) trail. Another long option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21955050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atzeneta del Maestrat – Mas de la Parra loop from Atzeneta del Maestrat&lt;/a&gt;, covering 32.2 miles (51.9 km).

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several natural attractions. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penyagolosa Summit&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4154736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de la Pegunta&lt;/a&gt; forest, or even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5526642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carbo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3437230" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de la Tosca&lt;/a&gt; is another interesting natural monument.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Xodos / Chodos?
A: The mountain bike trails around Xodos / Chodos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars. Riders often praise the challenging nature of the routes and the beautiful mountainous scenery.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see while mountain biking?
A: While primarily known for its natural beauty, you can find landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5714710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Joan de Penyagolosa Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, a significant religious building, which can be incorporated into longer rides or visited separately.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in this area?
A: Trail durations vary significantly based on distance and elevation. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931968043/parque-natural-de-penyagolosa-circular-desde-xodos-hasta-puertomingalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de Penyagolosa — Circular desde Xodos hasta Puertomingalvo&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 5 hours 40 minutes, while the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31448745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra d'en Galceran – Collado de la Bandereta loop from Atzeneta del Maestrat&lt;/a&gt; can take about 7 hours 40 minutes.

Q: Are there any mountain passes or challenging climbs for advanced riders?
A: Yes, the region is known for its challenging climbs and passes. Notable mountain passes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2809664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Port del Collao (Coll del Vidre)&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3890802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xodos / Chodos Pass (Atzeneta Side)&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3890819" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Bassa (Atzeneta Side)&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4037654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal Ascent in Parc Natural de Penyagolosa&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant climb.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Xodos / Chodos?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and potential for varied weather, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Xodos / Chodos, with milder temperatures and stable weather. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring colder temperatures and snow at higher elevations.
tips: Within the Natural Park of the same name is this emblematic Sanctuary to which pilgrimages take place year after year since the Middle Ages. A site that deserves to visit not only for its meaning but also for the beautiful environment that surrounds it!
